# Global Agent Instructions

You are an autonomous coding agent running in a headless orchestration session.
There is no human in the loop — do not ask questions or wait for input.

## Ground rules

1. Read and follow the project's CLAUDE.md for coding conventions and standards.
2. Never use interactive commands, slash commands, or plan mode.
3. Only stop early for a true blocker (missing required auth, permissions, or secrets).
   If blocked, post the blocker details as a Linear comment and stop.
4. Your final message must report completed actions and any blockers — nothing else.

## Execution approach

- Spend extra effort on planning and verification.
- Read all relevant files before writing code.
- When planning: read CLAUDE.md, the existing code in the area you are modifying, and any related docs.
- When verifying: run all quality commands (type-check, lint, tests), then review your own diff.
- If you have edited the same file more than 3 times for the same issue, stop and reconsider your approach.

## Session startup

Before starting any implementation work:

1. Run the project's type-check command to verify the codebase compiles clean.
2. Run the project's test command to verify all tests pass.
3. If either fails, investigate and fix before starting new work.

## Linear workpad

Use a single Linear comment as a persistent workpad:

- Title: `## Workpad`
- Update it at each milestone with: current status, decisions made, and next steps.
- On rework runs, append the rework section — do not delete prior content.

## Rework awareness

Every prompt in this workflow serves both first-run and rework cases.
On rework runs, the workspace already contains prior work.  Check for:

- An existing feature branch (do not create a new one)
- An open PR (push to it, do not open a second)
- Review comments requesting changes (address them specifically)
- Prior workpad content (append to it, do not overwrite)

# Investigation Stage

You are investigating issue ****: 

**Current status:** 
**Labels:** 
**URL:** 

## Issue description


No description provided.


## Objective

Understand the problem thoroughly before any code is written.  Your output is
an investigation summary posted as a Linear comment — not code changes.

## First run

1. Read the issue description and any existing Linear comments.
2. Identify the relevant source files — read them, understand the architecture.
3. If the issue is a bug: reproduce it first (run the failing test or repro steps).
4. If the issue is a feature: map out which files/modules need changes.
5. Write a structured investigation summary:
   - **Root cause** or **Requirements** (depending on issue type)
   - **Affected files** with brief explanation of needed changes
   - **Risks or open questions**
   - **Proposed approach** (high-level, 3-5 bullet points)
6. Post the summary as a Linear comment titled `## Investigation`.
7. Update the workpad with investigation status.

## Rework run

If this is a rework run (the workspace already has investigation content):

1. Read the review feedback from Linear comments.
2. Read your prior investigation summary.
3. Address the specific feedback — expand analysis, correct mistakes, or
   investigate additional areas as requested.
4. Update the `## Investigation` comment with revised findings.
5. Append a rework note to the workpad.

## Do NOT

- Write implementation code.
- Create branches or PRs.
- Modify source files (reading is fine).

## Summary: CRM Implementation in Moltis

I've thoroughly explored the CRM-related code in this Rust workspace. Here's what I found:

### 1. CRM Types (Complete & Well-Designed)

**Location:** `/Users/samjp/Code/stokowski-workspaces/CHA-11/crates/crm/src/types.rs`

The crate defines four core domain types:

- **Contact** — A person/entity with fields:
  - `id` (UUID), `name`, `source` (channel like "telegram"), `external_id`
  - PII fields wrapped in `Secret<String>`: `email`, `phone` (auto-redacted in debug output)
  - `stage: ContactStage` (enum: Lead, Prospect, Active, Inactive, Closed)
  - `metadata: serde_json::Value`, `created_at`, `updated_at` (milliseconds)

- **ContactChannel** — Maps a contact to external platforms:
  - `id`, `contact_id`, `channel_type`, `channel_id`, `display_name`, `verified`
  - Allows one contact to have multiple channel identities (Telegram, WhatsApp, Slack, etc.)

- **Matter** — Legal case/work item:
  - `id`, `contact_id` (nullable, ON DELETE SET NULL), `title`, `description`
  - `status: MatterStatus` (Open, OnHold, Closed, Archived)
  - `phase: MatterPhase` (Intake, Discovery, Negotiation, Resolution, Review, Closed)
  - `practice_area: PracticeArea` (Corporate, Employment, FamilyLaw, Immigration, IntellectualProperty, Litigation, RealEstate, Tax, Other)

- **Interaction** — Recorded touchpoint:
  - `id`, `contact_id` (required, ON DELETE CASCADE), `matter_id` (optional)
  - `kind: InteractionKind` (Call, Email, Message, Meeting, Note, Document)
  - `summary`, `channel`, `created_at`, `updated_at`

All types implement full serde serialization with enum-to-string conversions (e.g., `as_str()`, `FromStr`), and have comprehensive constructor methods (`.new()`, `.with_source()`, etc.).

### 2. `moltis-crm` Crate Structure

**Location:** `/Users/samjp/Code/stokowski-workspaces/CHA-11/crates/crm/`

The crate contains:
- `lib.rs` — Exports public API and `run_migrations()` function
- `types.rs` — Domain types (617 lines with extensive tests)
- `error.rs` — `Error` enum using `thiserror`, `Result<T>` type alias
- `store.rs` — `CrmStore` trait (async, Send + Sync) with 17 methods covering CRUD for all 4 types
- `store_sqlite.rs` — Production SQLite implementation (462 lines) with:
  - Row structs (`ContactRow`, `MatterRow`, etc.)
  - `TryFrom` impls for enum parsing and Secret handling
  - All CRUD operations using parameterized queries (`sqlx::query_as`)
  - 50+ tests covering upsert, list, get, delete, foreign keys, PII roundtrips
- `store_memory.rs` — In-memory implementation using `RwLock<HashMap<String, T>>` for testing

**Cargo.toml:**
```toml
[features]
default = []
metrics = ["dep:moltis-metrics"]
```

### 3. SQLite Migrations

**Location:** `/Users/samjp/Code/stokowski-workspaces/CHA-11/crates/crm/migrations/`

Two migration files (timestamp-based):

**`20260317000000_init.sql`** — Base schema:
- `crm_contacts` table (id, name, source, external_id, metadata, created_at, updated_at)
  - Indexes on `updated_at DESC` and composite `(source, external_id)`
- `crm_matters` table (id, title, status, contact_id FK → contacts ON DELETE SET NULL, metadata, timestamps)
  - Indexes on `updated_at`, `contact_id`, `status`
- `crm_interactions` table (id, contact_id FK → contacts ON DELETE CASCADE, matter_id FK → matters ON DELETE SET NULL, kind, body, metadata, created_at)
  - Indexes on `contact_id`, `matter_id`, `created_at DESC`
- `crm_contact_channels` table (id, contact_id FK ON DELETE CASCADE, channel_type, channel_identifier, verified, created_at)
  - UNIQUE constraint on `(channel_type, channel_identifier)`
  - Index on `contact_id`

**`20260317000001_crm_types.sql`** — Enrichment:
- Adds `email`, `phone`, `stage` columns to `crm_contacts`
- Renames `channel_identifier` → `channel_id` in `crm_contact_channels`, adds `display_name`, `updated_at`
- Adds `description`, `phase`, `practice_area` to `crm_matters`
- Renames `body` → `summary` in `crm_interactions`, adds `channel`, `updated_at`
- Rebuilds indexes after ALTER TABLE operations

Pattern: Uses `IF NOT EXISTS` and `ALTER TABLE` for idempotent migrations.

### 4. SQLite Store Pattern (Multi-Crate Pattern)

This project follows a consistent pattern across crates:

**Example 1: moltis-projects**
- `lib.rs` exports `run_migrations()` → calls `sqlx::migrate!("./migrations")`
- `store.rs` defines the `ProjectStore` trait
- Concrete impls: `SqliteProjectStore`, `TomlProjectStore`
- Migrations in `crates/projects/migrations/`

**Example 2: moltis-sessions**
- `lib.rs` exports `run_migrations()`
- Has foreign key dependency on projects (documented in comment)
- Migrations create `sessions` and `channel_sessions` tables

**CRM follows the same pattern:**
- `lib.rs` with `pub async fn run_migrations(pool: &sqlx::SqlitePool) -> Result<()>`
- `store.rs` defines `CrmStore` trait (abstraction)
- `store_sqlite.rs` implements it
- `store_memory.rs` for tests
- Migrations are idempotent with `set_ignore_missing(true)`

### 5. Config Schema & CRM Config

**Location:** `/Users/samjp/Code/stokowski-workspaces/CHA-11/crates/config/src/schema.rs`

`MoltisConfig` struct (line 226) includes:
```rust
pub crm: CrmConfig,
```

`CrmConfig` (lines 1049–1068):
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(default)]
pub struct CrmConfig {
    pub enabled: bool,  // Defaults to false
    pub auto_create_contacts: bool,  // Defaults to true
    pub default_practice_area: String,  // Valid: "corporate", "employment", etc.; defaults to "other"
}
```

### 6. Migration Initialization in Gateway

**Location:** `/Users/samjp/Code/stokowski-workspaces/CHA-11/crates/gateway/src/server.rs` (lines 1840–1876)

Migrations run in dependency order at startup (after sqlite pool creation):
```rust
moltis_projects::run_migrations(&db_pool).await.expect(...);
moltis_sessions::run_migrations(&db_pool).await.expect(...);
moltis_cron::run_migrations(&db_pool).await.expect(...);
crate::run_migrations(&db_pool).await.expect(...);  // Gateway's own

#[cfg(feature = "crm")]
moltis_crm::run_migrations(&db_pool).await.expect("failed to run crm migrations");
```

**Feature gating:** CRM migrations only run if `crm` feature is enabled.

### 7. Feature Flags & Dependencies

**crates/gateway/Cargo.toml:**
```toml
[dependencies]
moltis-crm = { optional = true, workspace = true }

[features]
default = ["agent", "caldav", "crm", ...]  # CRM enabled by default
crm = ["dep:moltis-crm"]
```

**crates/crm/Cargo.toml:**
```toml
[dependencies]
async-trait, secrecy, serde, serde_json, sqlx, thiserror, tokio, tracing, uuid
[features]
default = []
metrics = ["dep:moltis-metrics"]
```

### Key Patterns & Observations

1. **Type Safety:** All enums (ContactStage, MatterStatus, etc.) implement `FromStr` + `Display` + `as_str()`. Parsing errors are caught at SQLite→Rust conversion layer.

2. **PII Protection:** `Secret<String>` wraps email/phone; custom serde helpers ensure `expose_secret()` only called at consumption boundaries. Debug impl redacts output.

3. **Foreign Keys:** Cascading deletes (Contact deletion cascades to Interactions), nullable refs (Matters can exist without contacts).

4. **Async All the Way:** Everything is `async`, uses `async_trait`, `sqlx::query_as` with no blocking.

5. **Idempotent Migrations:** `set_ignore_missing(true)` + `IF NOT EXISTS` + `ALTER TABLE` make migrations safe to re-run.

6. **Trait-Based Abstraction:** `CrmStore` trait allows testing with `MemoryCrmStore` without SQLite, production with `SqliteCrmStore`.

7. **Indexed for Query Performance:** Composite indexes on lookups (source + external_id), temporal ordering (updated_at DESC).

8. **Workspace Dependencies:** All crates reference shared workspace deps (no version duplication in crate Cargo.tomls).

### 6. Migration Initialization in Gateway

**Location:** `/Users/samjp/Code/stokowski-workspaces/CHA-11/crates/gateway/src/server.rs` (lines 1840–1876)

Migrations run in dependency order at startup (after sqlite pool creation):

**Feature gating:** CRM migrations only run if `crm` feature is enabled.

### 7. Feature Flags & Dependencies

**crates/gateway/Cargo.toml:**
```toml
[dependencies]
moltis-crm = { optional = true, workspace = true }

[features]
default = ["agent", "caldav", "crm", ...]  # CRM enabled by default

```

**crates/crm/Cargo.toml:**

[dependencies]
async-trait, secrecy, serde, serde_json, sqlx, thiserror, tokio, tracing, uuid
[features]
default = []
metrics = ["dep:moltis-metrics"]
```

### Key Patterns & Observations

1. **Type Safety:** All enums (ContactStage, MatterStatus, etc.) implement `FromStr` + `Display` + `as_str()`. Parsing errors are caught at SQLite→Rust conversion layer.

2. **PII Protection:** `Secret<String>` wraps email/phone; custom serde helpers ensure `expose_secret()` only called at consumption boundaries. Debug impl redacts output.

3. **Foreign Keys:** Cascading deletes (Contact deletion cascades to Interactions), nullable refs (Matters can exist without contacts).

4. **Async All the Way:** Everything is `async`, uses `async_trait`, `sqlx::query_as` with no blocking.

5. **Idempotent Migrations:** `set_ignore_missing(true)` + `IF NOT EXISTS` + `ALTER TABLE` make migrations safe to re-run.

6. **Trait-Based Abstraction:** `CrmStore` trait allows testing with `MemoryCrmStore` without SQLite, production with `SqliteCrmStore`.

7. **Indexed for Query Performance:** Composite indexes on lookups (source + external_id), temporal ordering (updated_at DESC).

8. **Workspace Dependencies:** All crates reference shared workspace deps (no version duplication in crate Cargo.tomls).

1→//! CRM (Contact Relationship Management) for moltis.
     2→//!
     3→//! Provides domain types and persistent stores for tracking people and entities
     4→//! that interact with the system across any channel.
     5→//!
     6→//! # Core types
     7→//!
     8→//! - [`Contact`] — a person or entity, optionally with PII fields (`email`, `phone`)
     9→//! - [`ContactChannel`] — a channel identity linking a contact to an external platform
    10→//! - [`Matter`] — a legal case or work item linked to a contact
    11→//! - [`Interaction`] — a recorded touchpoint between the system and a contact
    12→//!
    13→//! # Enums
    14→//!
    15→//! - [`ContactStage`] — contact lifecycle stage
    16→//! - [`MatterStatus`] / [`MatterPhase`] — matter lifecycle state and phase
    17→//! - [`PracticeArea`] — legal practice area
    18→//! - [`InteractionKind`] — type of interaction
    19→
    20→pub mod error;
    21→pub mod store;
    22→pub mod store_memory;
    23→pub mod store_sqlite;
    24→pub mod types;
    25→
    26→pub use {
    27→    error::{Error, Result},
    28→    store::{CrmStore, MemoryCrmStore, SqliteCrmStore},
    29→    types::{
    30→        Contact, ContactChannel, ContactStage, Interaction, InteractionKind, Matter, MatterPhase,
    31→        MatterStatus, PracticeArea,
    32→    },

    35→/// Run database migrations for the CRM crate.
    36→///
    37→/// Creates and updates the `crm_contacts`, `crm_matters`, `crm_interactions`,
    38→/// and `crm_contact_channels` tables with indexes. Should be called at
    39→/// application startup before using [`SqliteCrmStore`].
    40→pub async fn run_migrations(pool: &sqlx::SqlitePool) -> Result<()> {
    41→    sqlx::migrate!("./migrations")
    42→        .set_ignore_missing(true)
    43→        .run(pool)
    44→        .await?;
    45→    Ok(())
    46→}

-- CRM tables
-- Owned by: moltis-crm crate

-- ── crm_contacts ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_contacts (
    id          TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,
    name        TEXT    NOT NULL,
    source      TEXT,
    external_id TEXT,
    metadata    TEXT,
    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,
    updated_at  INTEGER NOT NULL
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_contacts_updated_at
    ON crm_contacts(updated_at DESC);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_contacts_source_external_id
    ON crm_contacts(source, external_id)
    WHERE source IS NOT NULL AND external_id IS NOT NULL;

-- ── crm_matters ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- Matters represent cases, deals, or topics being tracked for a contact.
-- contact_id is nullable: a matter may not be tied to a single contact.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_matters (
    id         TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,
    title      TEXT    NOT NULL,
    status     TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open',
    contact_id TEXT    REFERENCES crm_contacts(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    metadata   TEXT,
    created_at INTEGER NOT NULL,
    updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_matters_updated_at
    ON crm_matters(updated_at DESC);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_matters_contact_id
    ON crm_matters(contact_id)
    WHERE contact_id IS NOT NULL;

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_matters_status
    ON crm_matters(status);

-- ── crm_interactions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- Logged interactions (messages, calls, meetings, notes) with a contact.
-- contact_id is required; matter_id is optional.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_interactions (
    id         TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,
    contact_id TEXT    NOT NULL REFERENCES crm_contacts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    matter_id  TEXT    REFERENCES crm_matters(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    kind       TEXT    NOT NULL,
    body       TEXT,
    metadata   TEXT,
    created_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_interactions_contact_id
    ON crm_interactions(contact_id);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_interactions_matter_id
    ON crm_interactions(matter_id)
    WHERE matter_id IS NOT NULL;

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_interactions_created_at
    ON crm_interactions(created_at DESC);

-- ── crm_contact_channels ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- Maps contacts to communication channels (Telegram, WhatsApp, Slack, …).
-- Each (channel_type, channel_identifier) pair belongs to exactly one contact.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_contact_channels (
    id                 TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,
    contact_id         TEXT    NOT NULL REFERENCES crm_contacts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    channel_type       TEXT    NOT NULL,
    channel_identifier TEXT    NOT NULL,
    verified           INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL,
    UNIQUE(channel_type, channel_identifier)
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_contact_channels_contact_id
    ON crm_contact_channels(contact_id);

const body = `## Investigation

### Current State

The \`moltis-crm\` crate already has substantial implementation from P1.2 (types) and P1.3 (config):

**Already done:**
- 4 domain types: \`Contact\`, \`ContactChannel\`, \`Matter\`, \`Interaction\` with rich enums (\`ContactStage\`, \`MatterStatus\`, \`MatterPhase\`, \`PracticeArea\`, \`InteractionKind\`)
- \`CrmStore\` trait with 17 async CRUD methods
- \`SqliteCrmStore\` — full SQLite implementation with parameterized queries, upsert-on-conflict, \`TryFrom\` row conversions
- \`MemoryCrmStore\` — in-memory impl for testing
- 2 idempotent migrations (\`20260317000000_init.sql\`, \`20260317000001_crm_types.sql\`) creating all 4 tables with indexes
- \`run_migrations()\` called at gateway startup (feature-gated \`#[cfg(feature = "crm")]\`)
- PII protection: \`Secret<String>\` for email/phone, \`ExposeSecret\` only at SQL boundary
- \`CrmConfig\` in schema.rs with validation in \`validate.rs\`
- Feature flags wired: \`crm\` feature in gateway and CLI Cargo.toml, enabled by default
- 53 passing tests (SQLite + memory store)

### Requirements from Issue Description

> CRUD operations for all 4 tables. Queries: list contacts (paginated, filterable by stage/practice_area), get contact with channels, list matters by contact, list interactions by contact/matter. Search by name/email/phone.

### Gap Analysis

| Requirement | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| CRUD for contacts | ✅ Done | upsert/get/list/delete |
| CRUD for contact_channels | ✅ Done | upsert/get_by_external/list_for_contact/delete |
| CRUD for matters | ✅ Done | upsert/get/list/list_by_contact/delete |
| CRUD for interactions | ✅ Done | upsert/get/list_by_contact/list_by_matter/delete |
| **Pagination** | ❌ Missing | All list methods return full \`Vec<T>\`, no offset/limit/cursor |
| **Filter by stage** | ❌ Missing | \`list()\` returns all contacts unfiltered |
| **Filter by practice_area** | ❌ Missing | \`list_matters()\` returns all matters unfiltered |
| **Get contact with channels** | ❌ Missing | No joined query — requires 2 calls today |
| **Search by name/email/phone** | ❌ Missing | No full-text or LIKE search on contacts |
| Gateway wiring (\`SqliteCrmStore\` in services) | ❌ Missing | Migrations run but no store instance in \`GatewayServices\` |
| \`tracing\` feature gate | ⚠️ Partial | \`tracing\` dep exists but no \`tracing\` feature flag (CLAUDE.md requires it) |
| API routes (REST/RPC) | ❌ Missing | No \`/api/crm/*\` routes or RPC methods |

### Affected Files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| \`crates/crm/src/store.rs\` | Add paginated/filtered list methods, search method, get-contact-with-channels |
| \`crates/crm/src/store_sqlite.rs\` | Implement new trait methods with SQL WHERE/LIMIT/OFFSET |
| \`crates/crm/src/store_memory.rs\` | Implement new trait methods for test parity |
| \`crates/crm/src/types.rs\` | Possibly add \`ContactWithChannels\` struct, pagination types |
| \`crates/crm/Cargo.toml\` | Add \`tracing\` feature flag |
| \`crates/gateway/src/services.rs\` | Add \`CrmStore\` field to \`GatewayServices\` |
| \`crates/gateway/src/server.rs\` | Instantiate \`SqliteCrmStore\` and wire into services |
| New: \`crates/gateway/src/crm_routes.rs\` or RPC methods | REST/RPC API for CRM operations |

### Risks and Open Questions

1. **Pagination strategy** — Offset/limit is simplest but cursor-based (\`updated_at\` + \`id\`) scales better. Other stores in the project don't appear to use pagination yet, so offset/limit is likely sufficient for now.
2. **Search implementation** — SQLite FTS5 exists in the memory crate for chunks. For CRM, simple \`LIKE '%query%'\` on name/email/phone may suffice initially vs. a full FTS5 table.      4→    Result,

     7→
     8→/// Persistence trait for CRM data.

    10→/// Provides CRUD operations for [`Contact`], [`Matter`], [`Interaction`], and
    11→/// [`ContactChannel`] records. Implementations include [`SqliteCrmStore`] for
    12→/// production use and [`MemoryCrmStore`] for testing.
    13→#[async_trait]
    14→pub trait CrmStore: Send + Sync {
    15→    // ── Contacts ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    16→
    17→    /// Return all contacts ordered by most-recently-updated first.
    18→    async fn list(&self) -> Result<Vec<Contact>>;
    19→
    20→    /// Return a single contact by ID, or `None` if not found.
    21→    async fn get(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Option<Contact>>;
    22→
    23→    /// Look up a contact by source channel and external ID.
    24→    async fn get_by_external(&self, source: &str, external_id: &str) -> Result<Option<Contact>>;
    25→
    26→    /// Insert or update a contact (upsert by ID).
    27→    async fn upsert(&self, contact: Contact) -> Result<()>;
    28→
    29→    /// Delete a contact by ID. Returns `Ok(())` if not found (idempotent).
    30→    async fn delete(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()>;
    31→

# Metrics and Tracing

Moltis includes comprehensive observability support through Prometheus metrics and
tracing integration. This document explains how to enable, configure, and use
these features.

## Overview

The metrics system is built on the [`metrics`](https://docs.rs/metrics) crate
facade, which provides a unified interface similar to the `log` crate. When the
`prometheus` feature is enabled, metrics are exported in Prometheus text format
for scraping by Grafana, Prometheus, or other monitoring tools.

All metrics are **feature-gated** — they add zero overhead when disabled.

## Feature Flags

Metrics are controlled by two feature flags:

| Feature | Description | Default |
|---------|-------------|---------|
| `metrics` | Enables metrics collection and the `/api/metrics` JSON API | Enabled |
| `prometheus` | Enables the `/metrics` Prometheus endpoint (requires `metrics`) | Enabled |

### Compile-Time Configuration

```toml
# Enable only metrics collection (no Prometheus endpoint)
moltis-gateway = { version = "0.1", features = ["metrics"] }

# Enable metrics with Prometheus export (default)
moltis-gateway = { version = "0.1", features = ["metrics", "prometheus"] }

# Enable metrics for specific crates
moltis-agents = { version = "0.1", features = ["metrics"] }
moltis-cron = { version = "0.1", features = ["metrics"] }
```

To build without metrics entirely:

```bash
cargo build --release --no-default-features --features "file-watcher,tailscale,tls,web-ui"
```

## Prometheus Endpoint

When the `prometheus` feature is enabled, the gateway exposes a `/metrics` endpoint:

```
GET http://localhost:18789/metrics
```

This endpoint is **unauthenticated** to allow Prometheus scrapers to access it.
It returns metrics in Prometheus text format:

```
# HELP moltis_http_requests_total Total number of HTTP requests handled
# TYPE moltis_http_requests_total counter
moltis_http_requests_total{method="GET",status="200",endpoint="/api/chat"} 42
